verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 is a type I integral membrane protein that cycles between the trans-Golgi network and the plasma membrane via clathrin-coated vesicles and endosomes. While primarily localized to the TGN at steady state, it has a documented plasma membrane pool reached through its own trafficking mechanism (CARTS carriers), not through transient recruitment. The protein anchors itself via its transmembrane domain throughout this cycle. This constitutive cycling pattern qualifies as dual localization with the PM being a functional minority compartment.
